law: For an app to perform any Wi-Fi Aware operations, it needs to declare the services that it intends to use for publishing and subscribing.
Before using Wi-Fi Aware in an app, it’s important to decide on the to use for the connections.
The first option is , the recommended option for almost all use cases.
The second option is , which is for instances that require low latency.
The sample app uses as it needs to send position updates for every frame of the simulation scene and thus requires very low latency.
To change the performance mode in the sample app, change the following line in : Using performance mode can drain a device’s battery, so use it only when it’s required.
The sample app uses the User Datagram Protocol (UDP) as the transport protocol because the use case doesn’t require reliable or in-order delivery.

Although this particular design of connecting to the first available endpoint works for the sample app’s use case, most apps typically review the discovered endpoint and make a connection only if required.
The setting provided during connection should match the one the app provides when starting the listener.
